Afternoon showers & thunderstorms return on Tuesday with slightly warmer (near record) temperatures lasting through Wednesday. A cold front brings a dose of widespread rain on Thursday morning, clearing out in the afternoon from west to east and potentially switching over to some brief flurries along the western escarpment.

Warm Sunshine Returns

Last Updated Mon March 09 at 10:40AM by Jack Kendrick

Today is the first of three more warm, sunny days in our extended early March warmup. A few spots of fog this morning clear out quickly, and we head for the upper 60s and low 70s in some spots. Afternoon showers & thunderstorms return on Tuesday with slightly warmer (near record) temperatures lasting through Wednesday. A cold front brings a dose of widespread rain on Thursday morning, clearing out in the afternoon from west to east and potentially switching over to some brief flurries along the western escarpment.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Mostly cloudy, with some clearing late; Morning rains changing to brief snow showers and flurries, especially west; Windy and much colder

Forecast Discussion

The sunshine is back in spades today, as upper-level ridging works back northward following our cooler, cloudier Sunday afternoon. A general west/southwesterly flow takes over for the next few days, sending warmth into the Carolinas.

Monday temperatures are warm, but Tuesday & Wednesday look to get us back into "record warm" territory. Tuesday's record in Boone is 69F, set back in 2000, and that record is likely to fall. A few scattered showers and thunderstorms will accompany this warmth, and that would be the wild card for potentially stopping temperatures from reaching warmer (as that big storm on Saturday did), but it won't change the overall story being exceptional warmth.

Wednesday should be drier than Tuesday, although we can't fully rule out a PM shower or storm. The big change comes Thursday morning, as a cold front sweeps through the area, bringing widespread morning showers. These will clear out west to east in the late morning hours, with only scattered rains left behind into the afternoon, although most are dry by then.

A few snow showers or flurries are possible along the western escarpment after the rain ends, but for most the story will simply be the sharply colder temperatures; 30 or more degrees colder on Thursday afternoon compared to Wednesday.

Temperatures rebound quickly, as we head back into the upper 40s and low 50s on Friday and the 50s and 60s on Saturday. Sunday brings similar warmth to Saturday, but another cold front is waiting in the wings to start next week.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 2, 2026

It's the first Monday Message for March! We discuss the chilly change for Monday and Tuesday, but the big weather story will be the spring fever that will be breaking out for the second half of the week.

Mar 02, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.
